about.howlDescription: Howl is a real-time communication platform for communities and friends.
about.howlFullDescription: Howl is a real-time communication platform for communities and friends. Create servers with text and voice channels, send direct messages, hop on voice and video calls, share your screen, and manage your community. All from one app. Built for speed and privacy.
about.legalPrefix: By using Howl you agree to our
about.legalTrademarks: All third-party trademarks (Google, Apple, Steam, etc.) belong to their respective owners and are used here solely to identify sign-in integrations.
about.madeWithHeart: Made with ♥ by the Howl team
about.openSourceDescription: Howl relies on several open-source projects. We're grateful to their maintainers and contributors.
about.technologyDescription: Howl is built with React, Vite, Express, Socket.IO, PostgreSQL, Prisma, and WebRTC.
about.technologyDescriptionGeneral: Howl is built with modern open-source tech. Encrypted messaging, low-latency voice, and cross-platform support. Every piece is picked for speed and reliability.
about.storyBody1: Howl isn't backed by venture capital or big tech. It's built by two friends who were tired of platforms that treat people as products. We don't harvest your data. We don't sell your info. We never will.
about.storyBody2: We set out to build the app we wanted to use: easy to set up, private by default, and no compromises on features. That's Howl.
about.valuesPrivate: Private by default. Your DMs, group chats, and calls are end-to-end encrypted, and by default only you hold the keys.
about.valuesNotForSale: Not your data for sale. No ad networks, no data brokers, no selling your information. Howl is funded by optional Pro subscriptions, not by mining you.
about.valuesFeatures: Features for everyone. Core communication is never paywalled. Pro adds flair, not access.
about.valuesIndependent: Independent. No VC, no big tech. Built by two people who answer to users, not investors.
about.securityIntro: Howl's private messaging is built on open standards, not homegrown crypto.
about.securityPqBody: Your conversations are protected by X-Wing, a hybrid key exchange that combines X25519 with ML-KEM-768 (NIST's post-quantum standard). Even a future quantum computer can't unlock the key exchanges it captures today.
about.securityMlsBody: DMs and group chats use the IETF's Messaging Layer Security standard for end-to-end encryption with AES-256-GCM, the modern protocol family designed for secure group messaging.
about.securityKeysBody: By default your keys are derived and stored on your device with Argon2id. Our servers store only ciphertext and can't read your DMs. Prefer recoverable backup? You can opt into encrypted recovery. It's your choice, and it's off by default.
about.securityFsBody: Keys ratchet forward continuously, so a single compromised key can't expose your past messages or, once healed, your future ones.
about.securityCallsBody: Voice, video, and screen-share are end-to-end encrypted. DM calls draw their keys from the same MLS group as your messages, so a call is as private as the chat it came from.
about.securityFilesBody: Files you upload in a DM are encrypted on your device with their own one-time key (AES-256-GCM) before they're stored. We keep only the ciphertext.
about.securityDevicesBody: Link more than one device and your message history follows you through an encrypted, cross-device archive, synced without ever exposing it to the server.
about.securityTransparency: A couple of honest notes. Messages in server and community channels are not end-to-end encrypted, so they stay visible to that server's moderators and communities can be kept safe. And GIFs from the GIF picker, link previews, and other embeds load from third-party providers, so they aren't end-to-end encrypted (the same is true in most messengers). End-to-end encryption covers your DMs, group DMs, uploaded files, and calls.

dm.encryption.deviceDesc: Auto-unlock encryption when you open Howl. Turn off on shared or public computers.
dm.encryption.disablePdExplainer: Howl deletes its backup of your keys, so from now on only you can unlock your messages. You will set a passphrase and save a recovery key.
dm.encryption.enablePdCon1: Howl can access your messages, since it holds the backup
dm.encryption.enablePdExplainer: Howl keeps a backup of your keys so you can get your messages back with just your password, the way most apps work. Because Howl holds the backup, it can access your messages.
dm.encryption.enablePdFailed: Incorrect passphrase or failed to enable
dm.encryption.enablePdPasswordHint: This is usually your account password, unless you changed it separately.
dm.encryption.enablePdPasswordLabel: Enter your encryption passphrase to continue
dm.encryption.enablePdPro1: Forget your password and your messages come back automatically
dm.encryption.passphraseTooShort: Passphrase must be at least 8 characters
dm.encryption.passwordDesc: This is separate from your login password. It protects your DM encryption keys.
dm.encryption.passwordHelper: This changes only your DM encryption password, not your account login.
dm.encryption.passwordPdDesc: Managed automatically. Synced with your account password.
dm.encryption.passwordSuccess: Encryption password changed. Save your new recovery key.
dm.encryption.pdModeDesc: When on, Howl keeps a backup of your keys so you can recover with just your password, the way most apps work, which means Howl can access your messages. When off, only you can recover them.
dm.encryption.pdModeWarning: You are using Self recovery: only you hold the key, so no one but you can read your messages. Keep your recovery key safe. If you lose it and your password, your history cannot be recovered.
dm.encryption.recoveryDesc: Used to recover your encrypted DMs if you forget your encryption password.
dm.encryption.recoveryEnterPassword: Enter your encryption password to regenerate
dm.encryption.recoveryKeySavePrompt: Save this recovery key somewhere safe. Your previous recovery key no longer works.
dm.encryption.recoveryPassphraseSet: Your recovery passphrase has been set. Remember it. Your previous recovery key no longer works.
dm.encryption.recoveryPassphraseTooShort: Passphrase must be at least 24 characters
dm.encryption.recoverySaved: I have saved my recovery key
dm.encryption.recoveryWarning: Your current recovery key will stop working.
dm.encryption.resetDesc: Permanently delete all DM encryption keys. Encrypted messages become unreadable.
dm.encryption.resetWarning: This will permanently delete all encryption keys. All your encrypted DM messages will become unreadable. This cannot be undone.
dm.encryption.statusLockedDesc: Enter your encryption password to manage settings.
dm.encryption.statusManualActive: Self recovery. Only you hold the key, so no one but you can read your messages.
dm.encryption.statusNotSetUpDesc: Set up a password to encrypt your direct messages end-to-end.
dm.encryption.statusPdActive: Server recovery is on. If you forget your password, Howl can restore your messages. Like most apps, that means Howl can access them.
dm.encryption.unlockOnLoginDesc: Ask for your encryption password right after logging in. Turn off to wait until you open DMs or answer a call.

dm.securePasswordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters
dm.secureRecoveryDescription: Save this recovery key somewhere safe. If you forget your DM password, this is the only way to recover your encrypted messages.
dm.secureRecoverySavedConfirm: I have saved my recovery key

dm.secureSetupPrompt: Set up end-to-end encrypted messaging. Only you and your recipient can read these messages.
dm.secureUnlockPrompt: Enter your DM password to unlock encryption.

emoji.hoverToPreview: Hover over an emoji to preview
encryption.chooseProtectionDesc: Pick how you want to protect your encrypted DMs
encryption.createPassphraseDesc: Create a passphrase to encrypt your messages. You'll need this passphrase on each device.
encryption.enterPassphraseDesc: Enter your passphrase to decrypt messages on this device.
encryption.howlCanAssist: Howl can recover your messages for you
encryption.maxPrivacyDesc: You hold the only key, so no one can read your messages but you, not even Howl. You will save a recovery key in case you forget your password. If you lose both your password and that key, your history cannot be brought back.
encryption.maxPrivacyTagline: Only you can unlock your messages
encryption.mlsLockedDesc: Encryption was paused on another tab. Reload to restore secure messaging.
encryption.passphraseMinLength: Passphrase must be at least 8 characters
encryption.recoveryInstructions: Enter your recovery key and set a new passphrase.
encryption.saveRecoveryKeyDesc: If you forget your passphrase, this key is the only way back into your messages. Save it somewhere safe. Howl cannot recover it for you.
encryption.savedRecoveryKey: I have saved my recovery key
encryption.secureEasyConfirmDesc: You are all set. Howl keeps a backup of your keys so you can always get back in, the way most apps work. This also means Howl can access these messages.
encryption.secureEasyDesc: The way most apps work. Howl keeps a backup of your keys, so if you forget your password we can bring your messages back for you, with nothing extra to save. Because we hold that backup, Howl can access your messages.
encryption.secureEasyTagline: Howl can restore your messages if you get locked out

settings.serverUpgrades.allSlotsInUse: All slots in use. Remove a power-up from a server to free up a slot.
settings.serverUpgrades.faqGetSlots: How do I get power-up slots?
settings.serverUpgrades.faqGetSlotsA: Power-up slots are included with Howl Pro. Pro subscribers receive 2 slots they can assign to any server they're a member of.
settings.serverUpgrades.faqHowPowerUp: How do I power up a server?
settings.serverUpgrades.faqHowPowerUpA: If you have Howl Pro, you get 2 power-up slots. Find the server you want to power up in the list above and click "Power up". You can move your power-ups between servers at any time.
settings.serverUpgrades.faqStackA: Each user can only apply one power-up per server, but multiple users can power up the same server to push it to higher tiers.
settings.serverUpgrades.faqTiersA: Tier 1 (2 power-ups) unlocks animated icons, better audio, and more emoji slots. Tier 2 (7 power-ups) adds HD streaming, an upload increase, and custom role icons. Tier 3 (14 power-ups) maxes everything out with 384kbps audio, 100MB uploads, and a custom invite link.
settings.serverUpgrades.faqWhatDoesA: A Power-up contributes to a server's power-up count. As a server accumulates power-ups it unlocks higher tiers, giving every member better audio quality, more emoji & sticker slots, higher upload limits, and other perks.
settings.serverUpgrades.noServersYet: You're not in any servers yet.
settings.serverUpgrades.perkSupporterIco: Stand out with a supporter icon in the member list
settings.serverUpgrades.perkUnlockTiers: Help your community unlock new tiers
settings.serverUpgrades.proBonusDesc: Howl Pro subscribers get 2 power-up slots included with their plan.
settings.serverUpgrades.purchaseDesc: Power-ups are {{price}}/month each. Buy as many as you want — they stack to unlock server tier perks.
settings.serverUpgrades.slotsAvailable: {{count}} slot(s) available — pick a server below to power up.

settings.serverUpgrades.upgradeToProDesc: Howl Pro includes 2 server power-up slots. Power up your favourite communities and unlock better perks for everyone.
settings.serverUpgrades.yourServersDesc: Choose which servers to power up with your available slots.

settings.voice.codecAutoDesc: Automatically selects the best codec for your hardware.
settings.voice.codecAv1Desc: Best compression efficiency. Requires RTX 40-series, RX 7000+, or Intel Arc for hardware encoding.
settings.voice.codecH264Desc: Universal support, lowest latency. Uses NVENC (NVIDIA), AMF (AMD), or Quick Sync (Intel) automatically.
settings.voice.codecVp9Desc: Better quality at lower bitrates. Good hardware support across modern GPUs.
settings.voice.dtxDesc: Reduces bandwidth during silence by sending fewer packets. Saves data but may cause slight clipping on speech onset.
settings.voice.fecDesc: Adds redundant data so the receiver can recover lost packets. Recommended for unstable connections.
settings.voice.forceSoftwareEncodingDesc: Disables GPU encoding and uses CPU instead. Higher CPU usage but can fix encoding issues on some systems. Requires restart.
settings.voice.inputLevelHint: Your input level is shown above in real time. Click "Let's Check" to hear how you sound to others.
settings.voice.preferredCodecDesc: Choose the video codec used when screen sharing
settings.voice.restartRequired: Restart required for this change to take effect.
settings.voice.showAdvancedStreamDesc: Stream Attenuation, Stream Attenuation Strength, and more.
settings.voice.showStreamPreviewsDesc: Allows others to see a preview of your stream before they join.
settings.voice.speakToSeeLevel: Speak into your microphone to see your input levels.
settings.voice.stereoDesc: Send two-channel audio. Uses more bandwidth but preserves spatial information. Best for music or instruments.
settings.voice.streamAttenuationDesc: Reduce the volume of other audio while you are streaming.
settings.voice.studioInfo: Mic is always open with all processing disabled. Opus is configured for high-fidelity stereo audio at 96 kbps.
settings.voice.upgradeEssentialBitrate: Upgrade to Essential for 128 kbps or Pro for 384 kbps
settings.voice.upgradeProBitrate: Upgrade to Pro for up to 384 kbps
settings.voiceIsolationDesc: Just your beautiful voice: let Howl cut through the noise. Best for noisy environments.
